name: Day-of Flight Cancellation — Get Onto the Next Workable Itinerary
description: Your flight just cancelled. Call the airline (or work the app + counter in parallel) to secure the next workable itinerary — including partner-airline and interline rebooks the front-line agent won't volunteer.

context.when_to_use: The user's flight has been cancelled or is delayed beyond a usable window (typically >3-4 hours), they are at the airport or near it, and they need to be at their destination on a specific date. Works for any commercial airline. If the cancellation is due to a published travel waiver, also load `irrops-waiver-awareness`.
preconditions: User has the cancelled flight's record locator (PNR / confirmation code) and ticket number (13-digit, starts with airline's 3-digit code e.g. 016 for UA, 001 for AA, 006 for DL).
User has the airline's elite/status phone number if they have status, or the general line.
User knows their hard constraints: must arrive by [date/time], acceptable connection points, willingness to fly into a nearby alt airport (e.g. EWR vs JFK vs LGA).
Phone in hand AND a spot in the gate-agent line — work both queues in parallel.

principles: Two queues at once: stand in the gate-agent line while you call. Whoever gets you first wins. Hang up the call if the counter solves it; release the spot in line if the call solves it.
Involuntary rebooking ('INVOL') unlocks options that voluntary changes do not — including other-airline (OAL) rebooks. Use the word 'involuntary' on the call.
Specific itineraries beat open questions. 'Can you put me on UA 718 SFO–EWR tonight, or AA 84 if that's full?' lands faster than 'what are my options?'.
Pre-shop the inventory yourself before calling. Use Google Flights, ExpertFlyer, or the airline's own search — know which flights have seats. Agents move faster when you hand them the answer.
HUACA — Hang Up And Call Again. If the first agent is unhelpful or insists nothing is available, politely end the call and redial. Different agent, different tools used, different result.
Foreign sales offices are open when US lines are jammed. UA Manila, DL India, AA Sydney, BA Newcastle all take US-itinerary calls and the queue is often 5 minutes vs. 90.

phrases.opener: Hi — my flight [flight number] on [date] was just cancelled. Record locator [PNR]. I need to be in [destination] by [time]. I've already looked at the schedule and I see [specific flight] has open seats — can you involuntarily rebook me onto that one?
phrases.invoke_invol: This is an involuntary rebook because the airline cancelled the flight. I'd like to use all involuntary options, including partner and interline carriers.
phrases.ask_oal: Since this is an INVOL, can you check OAL availability? On this route [carrier A] and [carrier B] both operate it and we have an interline agreement.
phrases.stall_research: Give me ten seconds to read out the flight numbers I'm comparing — I want to make sure we're looking at the same options.
phrases.elite_pivot: I'm a [status tier]. Can you transfer me to the [1K Voice / ConciergeKey / Diamond / 360] desk? They sometimes see inventory the main line doesn't.
phrases.huaca_close: Thanks for checking. Let me look at my schedule and call back. Have a good one.
phrases.graceful_close_success: Perfect. Can you read back the new flight numbers, seat assignments if any, the new record locator, and email the updated itinerary? Confirmation number for the rebook?
phrases.graceful_close_failure: Okay. Can you note on my PNR that I called and requested INVOL rebooking to [target flight], and that we couldn't reach it? I may pursue this further.

tactics.name: Pre-shop inventory before opening the call
tactics.script: Search Google Flights or the airline app for the same route over the next 24-48 hours. Note flight numbers, times, and which alliance/partner operates them. Have 3 acceptable options ranked.

tactics.name: Use the word 'involuntary'
tactics.script: Drop 'involuntary rebook' or 'INVOL' explicitly — it signals to the agent that change-fee waivers and OAL rebooking are on the table.

tactics.name: Ask for OAL / partner / interline by name
tactics.script: Name the alliance partner: 'United–Lufthansa' (Star), 'AA–BA' or 'AA–AS' (oneworld since 2024), 'DL–AF/KL/VS' (SkyTeam), 'AS–AA' (interline). Front-line agents often forget OAL rebooks exist until prompted.

tactics.name: Work two queues
tactics.when: At the airport when cancellation hits.
tactics.script: Get in the gate-agent line AND call simultaneously. The first one who solves it wins. Apologise and step out of whichever queue lost.

tactics.name: Escalate to elite / status desk
tactics.when: User has status and the main line stalls.
tactics.script: Ask for the dedicated tier line (1K Voice, ConciergeKey, Diamond Medallion, 360, AS 100K). They have wider discretion and shorter holds.

tactics.name: HUACA
tactics.when: Current agent says nothing's available and you have a known seat.
tactics.script: End the call politely. Wait 60 seconds. Redial. Different agent, often a different answer. Two HUACAs is fine; three is the limit before you switch tactics.

tactics.name: Foreign sales office
tactics.script: Call the airline's office in Manila / Sydney / Mumbai / Newcastle — they handle the same PNRs. Hold time is often single-digit minutes. Numbers are public on each carrier's site.

tactics.name: Accept a creative routing
tactics.script: Ask about: nearby airports (EWR ↔ JFK ↔ LGA, SFO ↔ OAK ↔ SJC, IAD ↔ DCA ↔ BWI), positioning flights (different connection hub), or split tickets the airline issues together.

boundaries: Do NOT accept a 'voluntary' change ticket where you pay fare difference — this is an INVOL rebook.
Do NOT cancel your existing ticket for a refund before securing a confirmed seat on the alternative. The original ticket is leverage.
Do NOT lie about status. Status is checked in real-time.
Do NOT book a separate ticket on a different carrier yourself without getting the airline to do an interline ticket — separate tickets break baggage protection and standby.

Do NOT accept an agent's 'this is the only option, you have to decide now' framing when your own inventory search shows seats elsewhere — say 'I'm looking at [specific flight] right now, can you check that one?' Agent inventory views differ from public search; the seat you see often is bookable.

Do NOT accept 'you'll forfeit your protections if you don't take this' as pressure. EU261 / UK261 / DOT 3-hour rule / contract-of-carriage rights attach to the cancellation event, not to your acceptance of a re-route. The protections survive a 'no thanks' to a bad re-route.
success_signals: Agent issues a new e-ticket and record locator on the spot.
Agent volunteers to check OAL inventory without being asked.
Agent mentions 'distressed passenger' or 'distressed pax fares' — that's the INVOL toolset opening up.
You're transferred to the elite desk.
failure_signals: Agent insists the only option is 'tomorrow's flight, same time' when other carriers clearly have seats.
Agent quotes a change fee or fare difference for an airline-caused cancellation.
Hold times exceed 60 minutes with no callback option.
Agent refuses to consider OAL despite a published interline agreement.
Agent pressures with 'this is the only option, decide now' while you can independently see other seats — manufactured-scarcity tell, HUACA or counter with the specific flight you found.
Agent claims accepting the bad re-route is required to preserve EU261/UK261/DOT compensation — false; compensation attaches to the cancellation, not to your re-route acceptance.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm new flight numbers, new PNR, seat assignments, baggage tag re-routing, and an emailed itinerary. If you've checked bags, confirm baggage rebooking out loud.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Note the PNR with the requested options. Pursue compensation separately (load `eu261-uk261-dot-compensation` skill). Consider buying a one-way on a third carrier and disputing later.
follow_ups: If the cancellation was carrier-controllable, file for hotel/meal vouchers (see `missed-connection-distressed-passenger`).
If EU/UK/Three-Hour Rule applies, file the cash compensation claim within 7 days while details are fresh.
Save boarding passes — required for both insurance and EU261 claims.

required_user_info: Cancelled flight number, date, and record locator (PNR)
Frequent flyer number and status tier (if any)
Acceptable alternate airports and connection points
